title: Set Date Column in Different Time Zone
excerpt: I’m using an automation like “When status changes to X, set Date column to today’s date.” It currently uses my time zone, but I need the date to reflect a different time zone. Is there a way to set the date column based …

posts.author: AGLwork (Aysegul)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m using an automation like “When status changes to X, set Date column to today’s date.” It currently uses my time zone, but I need the date to reflect a different time zone. Is there a way to set the date column based on a different time zone in automations?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: It depends on the user’s TZ setting. That is: the user that owns the automation. Automations integrations run on behalf of the user that created those automations / integrations. There is no native way to set the TZ for a specific date column.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it always the same time zone? Ie I live in Texas, but I can use AI to tell it to do my time plus 12 hours if I want it to show a different time zone.

image729×874 56.9 KB

posts.author: AGLwork (Aysegul)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks! That AI solution did the trick, problem solved
